---
title: List objects
subtitle: List objects and symmetric list objects
date: 22-04-2026
author:
  - name: Vikraman Choudhury
filters:
  - imagify
imagify:
  header-includes: \usepackage{quiver}
---

::: {.hidden}
$$
\newcommand{\blank}{{-}}
\newcommand{\cat}[1]{\mathcal{#1}}
$$
:::


Let $\cat{C}(1, \otimes)$ be a monoidal category, and $A$ be an object of $\cat{C}$.

A list algebra on $A$ is an object $L$ with a pair of maps $(1 \xrightarrow{n} L \xleftarrow{c} A \otimes L)$.

A list object on $A$ is a list algebra $(1 \xrightarrow{nil} L(A) \xleftarrow{cons} A \otimes L(A))$, such that for any parameterised list algebra $(P \xrightarrow{n} L \xleftarrow{c} A \otimes L)$, there is a unique map $it(n, c) : L(A) \otimes P \to L$ such that:
